mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase discount rates applied to equity cash flows, directly reducing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive 20-day returns dominate when FEDFUNDS rises.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate breaches the 5.0 percent threshold, it signals a deteriorating labor market that triggers immediate risk-off positioning, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in greater than 55 percent of instances where UNRATE exceeds 5.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in retail sales and housing starts confirm broad-based consumer demand expansion, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive changes in RSAFS and HOUST yield negative 60-day returns in greater than 60 percent of observations.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding industrial production reflects strengthening supply-side capacity and corporate output, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth fails to predict positive returns when CPIAUCSL annual growth exceeds 2.5 percent.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly inflation print exceeding 0.25 percent triggers immediate market repricing of future tightening expectations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL spikes above 0.25 percent MoM are followed by positive 5-day returns in greater than 50 percent of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained monthly growth in nonfarm payrolls signals robust economic momentum and rising corporate revenues, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows no statistical correlation with 60-day forward returns with Rank IC below 0.01 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent quarterly GDP expansion and monthly retail sales growth confirm synchronized demand strength, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive co-movement in GDPC1 and RSAFS yields negative 20-day returns in greater than 55 percent of triggered periods.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between declining industrial production and rising unemployment signals supply-side contraction and labor market weakness, triggering negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint decline in INDPRO and rise in UNRATE fails to predict negative returns when FEDFUNDS is below 0.5 percent.
